creator: sziszi@petra.hos.u-szeged.hu
text:
On Mon, May 07, 2001 at 10:20:08PM -0700, rgrace@aapt.com.au wrote:
> FreeBSD admnattest.vicone.net.au 4.2-RELEASE FreeBSD 4.2-RELEASE #0: Mon Nov 20
> 13:02:55 GMT 2000     jkh@bento.FreeBSD.org:/usr/src/sys/compile/GENERIC  i386
> >Description:
> After downloading 4.3-STABLE source via cvsup, make buildworld
> fails during the build of perl, with the following error:
> 
> ==> Your Makefile has been rebuilt. <==
> ==> Please rerun the make command.  <==
> 
> If I re-issue the ``make buildworld'' command, the source tree is
> cleaned, and the same error occurs.  I have been trying this each
> week since 4.3-RELEASE (I haven't got my subscription CDROM yet)
> without success.

Hello,

Please check that your computer's clock is working properly, similar errors
have been known to be caused by clocks that were drastically out of sync
with real time.

Hope this helps somewhat...
-- 
Regards:

Szilveszter ADAM
Szeged University
Szeged Hungary
